summary: solidity-deploy-guard exfiltrates host data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint at install and on execution
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 30) that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers process.cwd() and host identifiers, and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A sibling scanner.js extends this behavior: it walks the filesystem (fs.existsSync at lines 93/114), captures host identity via os.hostname() and execSync('whoami') (lines 233/234/246), and ships the results to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint over https.request/https.get (lines 142/143/161/323), with additional curl-based exfiltration paths (lines 298/323/408). The package name advertises a Solidity deployment safety check, but the shipped behavior is host reconnaissance and data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages domain — there is no benign reading of an install/runtime hook that POSTs cwd, hostname, whoami, and filesystem probes to a third-party static-hosting site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
